items.correct: The small projecting line at the end of letter strokes.
items.misconception: A whole typeface that looks formal.
items.why: Students conflate the FEATURE (the small foot/stroke) with the CLASSIFICATION (serif typefaces, which have them). Sans serif means 'without serifs' - those little projections at letter endings.

items.q: What were Impressionist painters primarily trying to capture?
items.correct: The fleeting effects of light and atmosphere at a particular moment.
items.misconception: Their feelings or first impressions of a scene.
items.why: Students take 'impression' as personal feeling. Impressionism was about visual perception - how light actually appears on surfaces, painted quickly outdoors before light changed.

items.correct: Strong contrast between light and dark to model three-dimensional form.
items.why: Students think it's about subject matter (night scenes). Chiaroscuro is a TECHNIQUE using value contrast to suggest volume - exemplified by Caravaggio but used since the Renaissance to model form.

items.q: Who painted Girl with a Pearl Earring?
items.why: Correct answer: Vermeer. “Da Vinci” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Warhol made Marilyn using which technique?
items.why: Correct answer: Silkscreen. “Oil paint” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many times is Marilyn's face repeated in the Diptych?
items.why: Exact answer: 50. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
